---
title: "The Nutcracker Suite"
classRating: "class5"
ydsRating: "8"
ydsSubRating: "none"
pitches: 5
parentArea: content/areas/manure-pile-buttress.mdx
---

## Overview

**Gear**

- Radios (comms on P5 can be a little tricky with the mantle)
- Nuts
- Cams as seen below
- 70m rope
- Shoes/pack for scramble walk down

0.30.40.50.75#1#2#322-42-42-4221

<RouteOverlay

## Approach

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/4CC0290E-FAAE-49C7-9E6B-2A6743F674F6-scaled1.TgbujAIy-768x1024.jpeg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:37,&quot;y&quot;:984},{&quot;x&quot;:282,&quot;y&quot;:881},{&quot;x&quot;:494,&quot;y&quot;:817},{&quot;x&quot;:549,&quot;y&quot;:799},{&quot;x&quot;:537,&quot;y&quot;:758}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## P1 - 5.7 or 5.9

### 5.7 original

Starts up a sort-of left-facing easy gully/crack system to the tree, then the upper half steepens into a low-angle lieback corner, and then over a lip. **Gear anchor, semi-hanging**.

**Link** with P2 (67m linked) to avoid hanging belay (just make sure to flip the rop over the corner).

![Start of P1](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/C149F5CC-4031-4296-93E6-0389258181731.3EofRoqy-768x1024.jpeg)
![Upper section layback](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/dsc026601.VwxLsEhE-1024x683.jpg)
![Top of P1](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/dsc026641.qXGxVaDr-1024x683.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/54E58D40-E33E-43ED-838D-E908EAE7AE9D-scaled1.qvGLvQPi-768x1024.jpeg)

### 5.9 variation

Go up the cool-looking (but seriously slippery) finger crack that angles right, then up.

## P2 - 5.4

Head up a low-angle corner crack, basically 3rd class, belay on a relatively **large ledge (with fixed slings?)**.

![Start of P2](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/A074B833-6E87-40EC-A9D3-A96BA96B9E2A-scaled1.eJONUVqH-768x1024.jpeg)
![End of P2](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/0C41AB15-5741-4A4F-910D-14BCDF889715-scaled1.MNj1EpKe-768x1024.jpeg)

## P3 - 5.7

Step right to gain a nice crack running up the wall. Climb to a stance below an overhang roof above. **Gear belay, semi-hanging**.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/4D71FF42-5CAD-4969-BC2C-DF1A369800C8-scaled1.2DUx1qPh-768x1024.jpeg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:755,&quot;y&quot;:704},{&quot;x&quot;:530,&quot;y&quot;:518},{&quot;x&quot;:444,&quot;y&quot;:459},{&quot;x&quot;:404,&quot;y&quot;:411},{&quot;x&quot;:421,&quot;y&quot;:338}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:4,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:2,&quot;x&quot;:420,&quot;y&quot;:332,&quot;w&quot;:18,&quot;h&quot;:18,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

![End of P3](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/dsc026721.80YEKiwG-1024x683.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/5C6C19EC-D823-48E3-BFDA-8177BC982793-scaled1.HoVHbQtj-768x1024.jpeg)

## P4 - 5.8, ~50m

Uses a lot of 0.4-0.75. Cut right on the roof, then traverse left on 5.8 slab to rejoin the crack system (old piton) before reaching the 5.8 hand-size splitter crack. Folllow a bit more 5.6 crack to a narrow ledge and **belay, semi-hanging**.

![Start of P4](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/BEBE3A85-9C39-415C-A3C8-A08F916B5E47-scaled1.eWe4Q7e-768x1024.jpeg)

## P5 - 5.8

Climb up to the corner system above and execute the infamous mantle (as long as you're 5'5" sounds fine). **Place a 0.4 above the mantle.** Continue above in a nice crack that takes you to a flat summit. The holds are pretty big and it's over quickly.

![Start of P5](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/B5DCAF31-517B-4F68-BA07-C0E9D35AB004-scaled1.EklyfKK-1024x768.jpeg)
![Top of P5](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/04/AC7195B5-2423-47C7-A362-F3586DDDD042-scaled1.aNtF2mRI-1024x768.jpeg)

## Descent

Scramble down off the back-left of the formation and down the gully back to the base.

The descent is mostly straightforward, fun, 5th class down-scrambling for a bit until you come to an obvious steep, rocky, switchbacked dirt climber's trail; easily follow the path back to the picnic area parking lot.
